title: Install Replacement Sensor Through Frame And Restart Operator Station
summary: Reconnect and secure a replacement sensor after it has already been routed through the frame, reinstall retained hardware and grommets, and then restart the operator station using the separately referenced startup procedure.
when_to_use: Use this procedure for the installation portion of a replacement sensor task after the replacement sensor has already been routed through the frame and the remaining documented actions are to reconnect, secure, restore removed grommets, and restart the operator station.
not_for: Do not use this runbook for the earlier sensor removal or routing steps; this excerpt begins after routing has already occurred.
Do not use this runbook as the full operator station startup procedure; the source directs the user to the separate procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66.
Do not use this runbook as a complete safety/setup procedure because the excerpt does not contain the full safety context for component replacement work.

responsibilities: Verify the replacement sensor has already been routed through the frame before continuing.
Reinstall the M3 button-head screw and any removed grommets.
Restart the operator station by following the separately referenced startup procedure.

safety_notes: This is component replacement work and the full safety/setup context is not contained in this excerpt.
Use the separate referenced procedure for restarting the operator station; this section does not provide those steps.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the routed replacement sensor
Access to the M3 button-head screw
Operator station restart procedure on page 66

access_required: Access to the routed replacement sensor
Access to the M3 button-head screw
Access to the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66
system_preconditions: The replacement sensor has already been routed through the frame.
external_references: "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66 of the same source manual.

visual_references.description: Maintenance diagram for the A-axis sensor PCA connections used during sensor installation.
Maintenance diagram showing the Z-axis CLINK sensor wiring connections during installation.
System screen artifact associated with stopping and startup procedures for the operator station.

steps.title: Verify sensor routing is complete
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented starting condition for this installation excerpt has been met before reconnecting and securing the replacement sensor.
steps.instruction: Verify the replacement sensor has already been routed through the frame before continuing with installation.
steps.expected_result: The replacement sensor is confirmed to be routed through the frame and ready for reconnection and securing.
steps.healthy_condition: The sensor is already routed through the frame as required by this installation excerpt.
steps.failure_condition: The sensor has not yet been routed through the frame, or routing status cannot be confirmed from the current work state.

actions.label: Confirm routed sensor position
actions.exact_action: Verify the replacement sensor has already been routed through the frame.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the replacement sensor has not already been routed through the frame.
Escalate if prior removal or routing steps are needed, because they are not provided in this excerpt.

source_evidence.text: Verify the replacement sensor has already been routed through the frame.
The excerpt begins mid-procedure and depends on prior removal/routing steps from the preceding section.

steps.title: Connect the M8 connection
steps.purpose: Reconnect the replacement sensor electrically/mechanically at the documented M8 connection point.
steps.instruction: Connect the M8 connection for the replacement sensor.
steps.healthy_condition: The M8 connection is installed and the replacement sensor is reconnected.
steps.failure_condition: The M8 connection cannot be connected, does not seat properly, or remains disconnected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: A-axis sensor PCA connection locations relevant to sensor installation and reconnection.
Z-axis sensor wiring connection diagram if the replacement sensor is a Z-axis sensor installation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the M8 connection cannot be positively identified from the current source context.
Escalate if the connection does not match the expected installation point or cannot be secured.

source_evidence.text: Figure 7-3, "A-Axis PCA Connections," appears on page 107 in section 7.3.2.1.2 Installation.
Figure 7-2, titled Z-Axis CLINK Connections, is on page 107 of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual in section 7.3.2.1.2 Installation under Sensors.

steps.title: Reinstall the M3 button-head screw
steps.purpose: Secure the replacement sensor in place after reconnection.
steps.expected_result: The replacement sensor is secured with the M3 button-head screw.
steps.healthy_condition: The M3 button-head screw is reinstalled and the sensor is secured in the frame.
steps.failure_condition: The M3 button-head screw is missing, cannot be reinstalled, or the sensor remains unsecured.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Maintenance diagram for the A-axis sensor PCA connections used during sensor installation, referenced as image support for reinstalling and securing the sensor.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the M3 button-head screw is unavailable or cannot be reinstalled.
Escalate if the sensor cannot be secured after reinstalling the screw.

source_evidence.text: Re-install the M3 button-head screw. [Image support needed: Maintenance diagram for the A-axis sensor PCA connections used during sensor installation.]
The nearby installation steps state that the sensor is fed through the wire routing hole on the rear side and fastened with an M3 button-head screw.

steps.title: Reinstall removed grommets
steps.purpose: Restore any removed protective or routing components associated with the sensor installation.
steps.expected_result: Any grommets removed during the preceding work are reinstalled.
steps.healthy_condition: Removed grommets are back in place.
steps.failure_condition: One or more removed grommets are not reinstalled or cannot be restored.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Lower grommet routing context for Z-axis sensor installation, if applicable to the replaced sensor.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if removed grommets cannot be identified or reinstalled.
Escalate if the installation cannot be restored to its prior routed/grommet condition.

source_evidence.text: The nearby text states that Z-axis wires route down through the column and out the lower grommet into the control box.

steps.title: Restart the operator station
steps.purpose: Return the operator station to service after the replacement sensor installation steps are complete.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is restarted by following the separate startup procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station restart is performed using the referenced startup procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The operator station is not restarted, or restart steps are attempted from this excerpt instead of the referenced procedure.

actions.label: Use referenced startup procedure
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System/operator station screen context associated with stopping and startup procedures for the operator station.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the separate procedure "Starting The Operator Station" is not available.
Escalate if operator station restart requires steps not contained in this excerpt and the referenced procedure cannot be followed.

source_evidence.text: Re-start the operator station using the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66.
Use the separate referenced procedure for restarting the operator station; this section does not provide those steps.

Page 82 of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual, section 5.4 stopping the operator station, contains a system screen artifact associated with nearby procedures for stopping the system and starting the operator station.

success_criteria: The M3 button-head screw is reinstalled and the sensor is secured.
The operator station is restarted using the referenced startup procedure.
healthy_conditions: The replacement sensor is routed through the frame before installation continues.
The sensor is secured with the M3 button-head screw.
The operator station restart is completed through the referenced procedure.
failure_conditions: The replacement sensor has not been routed through the frame before beginning this excerpted installation.
The M8 connection cannot be connected.
The M3 button-head screw cannot be reinstalled or the sensor remains unsecured.
The operator station restart procedure is unavailable or not followed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate when prior removal or routing steps are required, because this excerpt begins mid-procedure.
Escalate when the separate operator station startup procedure is needed but not available in the current work context.
Escalate if the sensor cannot be connected or secured with the documented hardware.
Escalate if full safety/setup context is required for the component replacement task, because it is not contained in this excerpt.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: A-axis sensor PCA connection locations and installation context, including the M3 button-head screw reference.
Z-axis wiring and lower grommet routing context if applicable to the replaced sensor.
Operator station/system startup screen context for the separately referenced restart procedure.

missing_details: The source excerpt text for page 109 is not present in the packet; step wording is derived from the candidate and attached evidence only.
The excerpt does not provide the detailed restart steps for "Starting The Operator Station"; it only references that separate procedure.
The excerpt does not provide complete safety controls, production stop requirements, or LOTO requirements for this installation segment.
